channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Officers who defended Capitol from rioters sue to block payouts from fund
item.description: Two police officers who helped defend the U.S. Capitol during the Jan. 6 riot are suing to block anyone from receiving payouts from a new settlement fund.
item.content:encoded:
Two police officers who helped defend the U.S. Capitol during the Jan. 6 riot are suing to block anyone from receiving payouts from a new settlement fund.

(Image credit: John Minchillo)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Ex-prosecutor charged with sending to herself report on Trump classified probe
item.description: The former prosecutor faces federal charges over allegations that she sent a report on Jack Smith's investigation into President Trump's hoarding of classified documents to her personal email account.
item.content:encoded:
The former prosecutor faces federal charges over allegations that she sent a report on Jack Smith's investigation into President Trump's hoarding of classified documents to her personal email account.

(Image credit: Julia Demaree Nikhinson)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Netanyahu scolds Israeli security minister for videos taunting flotilla activists
item.description: Israel's national security minister triggered a backlash after releasing videos taunting detained flotilla activists who tried to breach the blockade of Gaza, telling them they should be imprisoned.
item.content:encoded:
Israel's national security minister triggered a backlash after releasing videos taunting detained flotilla activists who tried to breach the blockade of Gaza, telling them they should be imprisoned.

(Image credit: Ohad Zwigenberg)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Ebola fears surge on the ground in Congo over rapid spread of a rare type
item.description: Healthcare workers in eastern Congo said Wednesday they are underprotected and undertrained in a rapidly spreading Ebola outbreak of a rare type of the virus in one of the world's most vulnerable places.
item.content:encoded:
Healthcare workers in eastern Congo said Wednesday they are underprotected and undertrained in a rapidly spreading Ebola outbreak of a rare type of the virus in one of the world's most vulnerable places.

(Image credit: Moses Sawasawa)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Liberal U.S. mayors team up with European counterparts to fight authoritarianism
item.description: Ten U.S. mayors from cities such as Chicago and Cincinnati have joined a pact with European mayors to defend democracy and progressive values and fight right-wing populists and authoritarianism.
item.content:encoded:
Ten U.S. mayors from cities such as Chicago and Cincinnati have joined a pact with European mayors to defend democracy and progressive values and fight right-wing populists and authoritarianism.

(Image credit: Courtesy of the city of Bratislava)
item.dc:creator: Frank Langfitt

item.title: Senate panel hears testimony on online sports betting, prediction markets
item.description: Senate lawmakers grilled sports betting industry officials during a hearing focused on recent cheating scandals, companies' marketing tactics and regulatory battles.
item.content:encoded:
Senate lawmakers grilled sports betting industry officials during a hearing focused on recent cheating scandals, companies' marketing tactics and regulatory battles.

(Image credit: Mariam Zuhaib)
item.dc:creator: Alana Wise

item.title: Advice for 2026 commencement speakers: Don't bring up AI
item.description: Commencement speakers who bring up the sweeping changes that artificial intelligence is driving are facing boos from the Class of 2026.
item.content:encoded:
Commencement speakers who bring up the sweeping changes that artificial intelligence is driving are facing boos from the Class of 2026.

(Image credit: University of Central Florida and Middle Tennessee State University via Storyful)
item.dc:creator: Jude Joffe-Block

item.title: Elon Musk's SpaceX IPO plans reveal blockbuster spending on rockets and AI
item.description: The company is on track to pull off the largest IPO in history — making CEO Elon Musk even wealthier.
item.content:encoded:
The company is on track to pull off the largest IPO in history — making CEO Elon Musk even wealthier.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Maria Aspan

item.title: Supreme Court voting rights ruling could play a big role at the local level
item.description: The Supreme Court's recent ruling threatens the power of racial-minority voters in Voting Rights Act cases about not just Congress, but also at least 17 state and local governments, NPR finds.
item.content:encoded: The Supreme Court's recent ruling threatens the power of racial-minority voters in Voting Rights Act cases about not just Congress, but also at least 17 state and local governments, NPR finds.
item.dc:creator: Hansi Lo Wang

item.title: How to help children cope after shootings like the San Diego mosque killings
item.description: Children closest to an incident of gun violence have the most risk of lasting psychological effects. Here's what all parents should know about how to buffer trauma.
item.content:encoded:
Children closest to an incident of gun violence have the most risk of lasting psychological effects. Here's what all parents should know about how to buffer trauma.

(Image credit: Gregory Bull)
item.dc:creator: Rhitu Chatterjee
